channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Kissing the ring? MSNBC 'Morning Joe' hosts say they met with Trump to reopen lines of communication
item.description: It was the last thing you might expect if you watched MSNBC's “Morning Joe” a month ago — hosts Joe Scarborough and Mika Brzezinski asking for a meeting and traveling to Florida to talk with President-Elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bolton: Moscow, Beijing should feel ‘a lot of relief’ if Gabbard is confirmed as DNI
item.description: Former national security adviser John Bolton said Russia and China should feel “a lot of relief” if former Democratic Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii) is confirmed as President-elect Trump’s director of national intelligence. “She’s not capable of doing it,” Bolton said Sunday on CNN.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Ethics Committee to meet Wednesday after postponing Gaetz investigation meeting
item.description: The House Ethics Committee is expected to meet this week after previously postponing a planned meeting on its investigation into now-former Rep. Matt Gaetz.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Size of slim Republican House majority hangs on 5 uncalled races
item.description: There are still eight uncalled House races that will determine the size of the Republican majority in Congress after the 2024 elections.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democratic AGs rush to form line of defense against Trump
item.description: Democratic attorneys general across the country are readying their legal defenses against the incoming Trump administration, preparing to pounce on potential violations and even take the president-elect to court if he implements controversial policies.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Carolina Democrats find electoral success further down the ballot and hope to build on it
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Democrats fell short again in wresting away swing-state prize North Carolina from Republicans in the presidential election, but scored significant downballot victories, giving them hope as they look to the future.
item.dc:creator: GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lara Trump emerges as an early favorite to fill Rubio's Senate seat
item.description: Appointing Lara Trump would ensure that the incoming president has a loyalist in the Senate, but it would most definitely come with criticism from Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: 1 in 5 US residents 'more likely' to move after election, according to Redfin
item.description: Of those who say they are now more likely to move, 36% say the election results are making them consider a different country.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Women stock up on abortion pills and Plan B, fearing new restrictions under Trump
item.description: Telehealth providers say requests for the pills have spiked since the election. Patients and doctors worry what a Trump presidency could mean for medical abortion and emergency contraception.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Boden and Elissa Nadworny, MPR News

item.title: Trump is creating team of loyalists after conflicts with Cabinet members in 1st term
item.description: In his second term, President-elect Donald Trump is surrounding himself with people who can amplify his message and agree with how he wants to remake the executive branch.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Trump should share Prince Harry immigration docs: Heritage Foundation
item.description: "Prince Harry is one of the most public figures in the world," Nile Gardiner of the Heritage Foundation said.
item.dc:creator: Blake Burman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump picks Karoline Leavitt to be youngest White House press secretary in history
item.description: Leavitt will be the youngest person to ever hold the job and is expected to deliver press briefings based on Trump’s statement late Friday that he has “utmost confidence she will excel at the podium.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: What’s ahead for abortion access in Minnesota under a Trump administration
item.description: While detailed plans have yet to emerge, health and legal experts, practitioners and advocates in Minnesota are closely monitoring the new administration for hints about what federal changes to abortion could mean for the state.
item.dc:creator: Erica Zurek, MPR News

item.title: The last actions the Biden administration will take before Trump takes over the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Biden administration officials are working against the clock doling out billions in grants and taking other steps to try to preserve at least some of the outgoing president’s legacy before President-elect Donald Trump takes office in January.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, MATTHEW DALY and COLLIN BINKLEY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Matt Gaetz is Trump's pick for attorney general
item.description: President-elect Trump announced Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) will serve as his attorney general, tapping a loyal congressional ally who himself was the subject of a Justice Department investigation.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
